behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in pure backoff math, not a module handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess is needed; deterministic inputs (attempt count, base delay, cap) can reproduce failures exactly. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Higher levels rejected: no behavior text requires real I/O; this is local policy computation.
behaviors.test_description: Test a single backoff-calculation function with table-driven cases (failures 1..N), asserting exponential growth and clamp at configured max delay.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is classification/parsing plus delay computation and can fail within one retry-policy function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: synthetic response objects/status+headers are sufficient; real HTTP is not required to trigger the logic bug. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Higher levels rejected by behavior text: it asks to "identifies a rate-limit response and computes" wait time, which is deterministic local logic.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative response fixtures (e.g., 429 with/without retry headers, non-429) into retry-decision logic and assert retry/no-retry and computed delay.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: Unit-floor evidence: "client actually resends the original request" and "returns the successful result to the caller transparently" require interaction between retry scheduler, transport wrapper, and call() return path. Ceiling-stop evidence: behavior targets local resend/return semantics, not real network faults; use in-process fake transport and fake clock, and there is no AI/model judgment in the text. REAL-INFRA check: real HTTP not required to catch resend/original-payload loss bugs. Higher levels rejected: no text requiring real OS/network timing conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire client + retry policy + in-memory fake HTTP transport + controllable clock. First response is rate-limited, advance clock by computed wait, verify original request is resent once and final success body is returned unchanged to caller.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: Unit-floor evidence: "after the configured maximum number of retries" plus "client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError`" crosses retry-counter/orchestrator and error-mapping boundaries. Ceiling-stop evidence: assertion is local type mapping ("typed ... rather than generic error"), which is testable with in-process stubs; no model decision/autonomy appears. REAL-INFRA check: real network is unnecessary because repeated stubbed rate-limit responses reproduce exhaustion path.
behaviors.test_description: Compose client with fake transport that always returns rate-limit responses; run until retry limit, then assert thrown/returned error is `QuotaExhaustedError` with expected metadata and not a generic Error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: Unit-floor evidence: "full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking" is multi-component orchestration/state handoff, not one function. Ceiling-stop evidence: focus is termination and error surfacing in client logic; this can be proven with in-process fake transport + fake timer, and no AI/model judgment is involved. REAL-INFRA check: no specific OS/network/subprocess behavior is cited. Higher level (System) rejected because behavior text does not require real HTTP/timeouts, only correct chain termination.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end client modules with deterministic fake clock and always-rate-limited transport; assert bounded attempt count, final exhaustion error surfaced, and no additional scheduled retries remain.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Unit-floor evidence: "multiple async callers" and "same shared quota bucket" requires boundary testing across caller tasks + shared quota state module. Ceiling-stop evidence: explicit "in the same process" supports in-process concurrency testing (no real distributed/OS boundary), and no model judgment exists. REAL-INFRA check: concurrency race bugs are catchable with local async scheduling and stub transport; real network is optional, not minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate one client/quota manager, launch concurrent `call()` promises with controlled scheduler, and assert all attempts decrement a single shared bucket (not per-caller buckets) under interleavings.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: Unit-floor evidence: "multiple requests are waiting" and "dispatched in the order ... submitted" requires queueing component plus dispatcher interaction. Ceiling-stop evidence: behavior is ordering policy in library internals and can be validated with in-process queue/dispatcher fakes; no AI/model output is part of correctness. REAL-INFRA check: real network/subprocess not needed to expose FIFO violations.
behaviors.test_description: Submit several calls while quota is unavailable, then release capacity stepwise in a fake quota/transport setup; assert dispatch sequence matches submission order exactly.
